rationale: The citing note explicitly positions the 2011 paper as extending prior studies on advice effects on demand that used aggregate outcomes and faced endogeneity concerns, while the cited 2006 paper is exactly an empirical sales-impact study of online word of mouth. Given this clear conceptual linkage (advice/reviews  demand effects) and no evidence that the citation is merely incidental beyond a bare reference match, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
